pressure.summary: AI tools reduced demand for first-draft problem structuring work. Junior analysts are now producing slide-ready outputs in hours that previously took days. The value of mid-level consultants who built careers on structuring ambiguous problems is compressing.

actions_taken: Started using AI tools in project scoping to stay ahead of junior output quality
Shifted focus toward client-facing synthesis and judgment-heavy deliverables
Began positioning internally as the person who knows how to direct AI rather than produce with it

outcome.early_effect: Work remains secure but expectations have increased. Clients now expect faster turnaround and higher polish. The role feels sustainable but the margin for error has shrunk.

narrative.short_context: I have 8 years in consulting. The first 6 were about learning to structure problems. The last 2 have been about learning to direct machines that structure problems. The skill set is shifting faster than I expected and the firm is not formally acknowledging it yet.
narrative.path_next_considered: Move toward AI-enabled strategy delivery roles — either within consulting or on the client side where I can own the AI transformation rather than support it.
